steps.title: Identify the variation across studies
steps.content: The three notes report three different outcomes: positive, neutral, and negative effects. Any valid generalization must account for this pattern of different results, not single out one study or falsely claim consistency.

steps.title: Match to the correct generalization
steps.content: Choice A directly states that effects can vary, reflecting the three distinct outcomes shown across the studies. The other three either contradict the data (C claims the 2017 study found negative effects; D claims consistent positive effects), reverse causality (B), or fail to synthesize across all studies. That matches choice A — A is correct.
